checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Regex changed from `[^.!?\s][^.!?]*[.!?]?` to `(?:[^.!?\s][^.!?]*[.!?]*|[.!?]+)` — captures runs of consecutive punctuation as trailing delimiters or standalone runs, exactly addressing the root cause. Tests cover all three reproductions plus a 10-input property-based reconstruction check.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only the two files named in the plan (chunk-text.ts and chunk-text.test.ts) were changed; the production fix is a single regex line plus comment updates, and the test additions are purely additive with no unrelated churn.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: Tests assert `chunks.join('') === original` and per-chunk `length <= maxLength` — real behavioral verification. No existing tests were weakened, deleted, or hard-coded; the regex fix addresses the genuine segmentation defect.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: The `[.!?]*` greedily captures trailing delimiters and `[.!?]+` handles standalone runs; edge cases (leading punctuation, no-space runs, mixed whitespace) all reconstruct correctly. Minor residual risk: standalone-delimiter alternative could produce many tiny segments for spaced-punctuation inputs, but packing still enforces maxLength. No regression risk for normal text.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

dimensions.evidence: Regex changed from `[^.!?\s][^.!?]*[.!?]?` to `(?:[^.!?\s][^.!?]*[.!?]*|[.!?]+)` — captures runs of consecutive punctuation as trailing delimiters or standalone runs, exactly addressing the root cause. Tests cover all three reproductions plus a 10-input property-based reconstruction check.

Only the two files named in the plan (chunk-text.ts and chunk-text.test.ts) were changed; the production fix is a single regex line plus comment updates, and the test additions are purely additive with no unrelated churn.

Tests assert `chunks.join('') === original` and per-chunk `length <= maxLength` — real behavioral verification. No existing tests were weakened, deleted, or hard-coded; the regex fix addresses the genuine segmentation defect.

The `[.!?]*` greedily captures trailing delimiters and `[.!?]+` handles standalone runs; edge cases (leading punctuation, no-space runs, mixed whitespace) all reconstruct correctly. Minor residual risk: standalone-delimiter alternative could produce many tiny segments for spaced-punctuation inputs, but packing still enforces maxLength. No regression risk for normal text.
